design_philosophy.core_loop: Use magical rattle to inflate enemies into temporary platforms or weapons; navigate levels by stacking inflated enemies or pushing them to damage other foes
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (health via milk bottles) combined with creative enemy manipulation puzzle-solving
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as baby Upa wielding a magical rattle to save the kingdom from demon Zai by rescuing kidnapped babies and defeating enemies through clever platforming
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unique dual-use enemy mechanic: inflated enemies function as both traversal platforms (with time limits) and moveable projectiles. Emphasizes creative problem-solving over reflex execution. Baby protagonist wielding rattle as sole weapon is unconventional for the era.

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives system; FDS version starts with 2 lives on Normal difficulty, 4 lives on Easy. Famicom cartridge adds explicit Easy/Normal difficulty choice at start screen
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with health meter restoration between levels; milk bottles scattered throughout levels restore health mid-stage
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Famicom cartridge version introduced two explicit difficulty modes: Easy (3 starting health, 4 lives, reduced enemy damage) vs Normal (2 health, 2 lives, equivalent to original FDS). Players can choose at title screen.

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups: heart icons increase max health for current world; bells grant invincibility and running ability; hourglasses freeze enemies; scorpion blocks clear screen. Milk bottles restore health.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden level select accessible via controller input cheat (Up+A on Controller 1, Down+B on Controller 2, then Select)
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select for direct stage access
Difficulty modes (Easy vs Normal) in Famicom version offer different challenge curves
Multiple release versions (FDS vs Famicom cartridge) with audio/visual differences

world_building_techniques: Opening story exposition: Upa is prince of magical kingdom; demon Zai escaped from urn, kidnapped babies; fairy granted Upa magical rattle to save kingdom
Character design: baby protagonist (unconventional hero archetype)
Environmental theming: magical kingdom setting, cake-filled stages, underwater segments suggesting varied fantasy domains
Guest appearances in other Konami games reinforce character in broader universe

faction_indicators: Upa's lineage as prince of magical kingdom establishes royal faction
Demon Zai as corrupting external force threatening kingdom stability
danger_signals: Life force draining by Zai indicating systemic threat
Mass kidnapping of babies (except Upa) suggesting vulnerability of civilian population
Urn-bound demon representing contained threat that became unleashed
environmental_storytelling: Cake-filled stages suggesting surreal, whimsical kingdom aesthetics
Underwater segments indicating diverse terrain within kingdom borders
Item blocks featuring Upa's face reinforcing protagonist as kingdom figure
Magical rattle as symbol of hope granted by imprisoned fairy
